title: VerifierNodeRegistry — Android verifier node lifecycle (verifier D, Faza 2)
scope: one machine instance per node signing key (StrongBox/TEE secp256k1 address); the contract multiplexes all of them

xDAI.note: native Gnosis coin staked by node operators (anti-spoof economy, docs/18) — amounts are an open decision

states.label: Pending (DAO vote)
states.guard: status == Pending — registered + staked, hardware attestation awaiting off-chain DAO verification

states.label: Active (co-signer)
states.guard: status == Active — isActive() true; only now do the node's EIP-712 signatures count in MeshVerifier

states.label: Offline
states.guard: status == Offline — signatures rejected; stake withdrawable after unstakeDelay

states.label: Ejected (terminal)
states.guard: status == Ejected — stake slashed to treasury; the signing key is burned forever (cannot re-register)

transitions.label: apply + stake
guards: signingKey != 0, not seen before (Ejected keys can never return)
attestationRef != 0 (hash of Android Key Attestation evidence — verified OFF-chain by the DAO)

money.note: stake >= minStake locked in the registry

transitions.label: top up stake (self-loop)
transitions.note: Representative edge — addStake is allowed in Pending, Active and Offline alike (e.g. to re-qualify after governance raised minStake).

transitions.label: DAO admission
transitions.caller: governance (after off-chain verification of the HW attestation + DAO vote, docs/18)
governance OR the node's own operator (voluntary downtime)
transitions.note: MeshVerifier's live check makes the node's signatures invalid in the same block.

transitions.label: reactivation
transitions.note: Otvorena odluka: whether reactivation should require fresh hardware attestation — docs are silent, allowed without it here.

transitions.label: exit with stake (self-loop)
guards: block.timestamp >= statusChangedAt + unstakeDelay (a misbehaving node cannot exit faster than the DAO can eject)

money.note: full stake returned; record stays Offline with stake 0
entire stake — slashing formula is an open decision
still slashable while the unstake delay runs

notes: Doc-specified lifecycle (docs/18, whitepaper §5): pending → active → offline → ejected; DAO (Safe multisig) admits and kicks; known human operator per node.

ONCHAIN: signing key, operator, status, stake, attestationRef (hash of the Android Key Attestation evidence). OFF-chain: Play Integrity / Key Attestation verification, Certilia OIDC validation, pepper custody (ADR 0003), Cloudflare Tunnel, geolocation map.
MeshVerifier.verify() checks isActive(signer) LIVE per signature — there is no cached signer set to desynchronize; ejection is instantaneous censorship of a node's signatures.

activeNodeCount is the ceiling for MeshVerifier.setThreshold (ThresholdUnsatisfiable) — but the registry does not know the mesh threshold, so DAO ejections can push the count below M; verify() then fails closed until the DAO adjusts.
Otvorene odluke (see contract NatSpec): minStake amount, slashing formula (full stake here), proof-of-location integration, Acurast substrate, re-attestation on reactivation, unstakeDelay length.
